mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is falling, discount rates ease and equity valuations re-rate upward. A sustained decline should predict positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days because lower short rates reduce the present-value drag on expected cash flows.
failure_condition: Rejected if 20-day SPX returns are not positive in the majority of periods when FEDFUNDS is trending down, or if the rolling 3-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in FEDFUNDS above a restrictive threshold tightens financial conditions, compresses risk appetite, and raises recession odds. Once the policy rate is high enough, the next 20 trading days should exhibit negative S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Rejected if triggered observations produce positive 20-day SPX returns more than 60% of the time, or if the sign of the effect is not negative in a tightening regime.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE signals weakening labor income and higher near-term recession risk, which should pressure cyclical earnings and equity prices. The effect should manifest over roughly one month as investors price slower aggregate demand.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3pp over 3 months
failure_condition: Rejected if UNRATE-rise triggers are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 55% of cases, or if the signal's rolling IC is indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS and PAYEMS both accelerate, household income and retail activity are improving together, which supports revenue growth and should lift stocks over the next 60 trading days. The joint confirmation matters because demand strength in spending and employment is more informative than either series alone.
failure_condition: Rejected if the joint condition does not outperform either single proxy, or if positive 60-day SPX returns do not occur more often when both series rise together.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in INDPRO indicates stronger industrial output and better real activity momentum, which should support equities through earnings revisions over the next quarter. The signal is strongest when production is not already reversing after a transient rebound.
failure_condition: Rejected if positive INDPRO momentum is not associated with positive 60-day SPX returns, or if the effect disappears once the 3-month trend turns negative.

mechanism_narrative: A drop in HOUST below prior trend indicates housing demand deterioration and a broader slowdown in rate-sensitive spending. Because housing transmits to consumer confidence and related industries with a lag, the next 20 trading days should be negative for the S&P 500.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below its 6-month moving average
failure_condition: Rejected if downside HOUST breaks are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns more than 55% of the time, or if the effect vanishes in rate-sensitive periods.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of high CPIAUCSL inflation and low FEDFUNDS implies policy is behind the inflation curve, raising the odds of future tightening and multiple compression. This interaction should be bearish for S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rejected if high-inflation/low-rate combinations do not underperform baseline SPX returns, or if the interaction term is not negative in rolling walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery, falling unemployment together with rising payrolls confirms that labor slack is being absorbed and household income is improving. Under that regime, S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days should be positive because earnings expectations broaden beyond defensive sectors.
failure_condition: Rejected if falling UNRATE plus rising PAYEMS fails to produce above-baseline 60-day SPX returns, or if the joint signal loses predictive power outside recovery phases.
